task_completion.description: Add a Pressure Relief Valve (PSV-101) to the tank top
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pressure relief/safety valve symbol is placed on top of the Methanol Storage Tank T-100 and is explicitly labeled with the tag 'PSV-101'.

task_completion.description: Add a Centrifugal Pump (P-101) on the outlet line
task_completion.visual_evidence: A centrifugal pump symbol is placed on the outlet line exiting the tank and is explicitly labeled with the tag 'P-101'.

task_completion.description: Add a Check Valve (V-102) downstream of the pump
task_completion.visual_evidence: A check valve symbol is placed on the line after the pump and is explicitly labeled with the tag 'V-102'.

task_completion.description: Add a Shell and Tube Heat Exchanger (E-101) downstream of the check valve
task_completion.visual_evidence: A shell and tube heat exchanger symbol is placed on the line after the check valve and is explicitly labeled with the tag 'E-101'.

task_completion.description: Connect the components to show the correct flow sequence
task_completion.visual_evidence: Connecting lines clearly show the flow path in this exact order: Tank -> Pump (P-101) -> Check Valve (V-102) -> Heat Exchanger (E-101) -> Exit.

task_completion.description: Export the updated diagram as a PDF
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io 'Export as PDF' dialog is used, and the file is saved as 'methanol_storage_rev1.pdf' in the '~/Diagrams/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the draw.io graphical interface to edit the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io (diagrams.net) application open with the diagram being visually edited using the GUI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the GUI by editing the .drawio XML file directly
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) are shown editing the raw XML code of 'methanol_storage.drawio'.

integrity.description: Verify the PDF was genuinely exported from draw.io
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the 'File -> Export as -> PDF' menu in draw.io, rather than using terminal commands to create a fake PDF file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required equipment tags are PSV-101 (Pressure Safety Valve), P-101 (Pump), V-102 (Check Valve), and E-101 (Exchanger). These follow standard ISA P&ID naming conventions. The components should be drawn using standard P&ID symbols (e.g., from the draw.io mxgraph.pid library). The export file must be named 'methanol_storage_rev1.pdf' and located in '/home/ga/Diagrams/'.
